recipes.name: Koshari
recipes.description: Egypt's national dish and most beloved street food. Layers of rice, lentils, macaroni, and chickpeas topped with crispy fried onions and zesty tomato sauce create a hearty, satisfying vegetarian meal that has been enjoyed for generations.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Rinse the lentils and cook in boiling water for 20 minutes until tender but not mushy. Drain and set 6 Main Courses
Cook the rice according to package directions and set aside.
Cook the macaroni until al dente, drain, and set aside.
If using dried chickpeas, soak overnight and boil until tender. If canned, rinse and warm through.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep pan. Fry the sliced onions in batches until crispy and golden brown. Drain on paper towels.
For the tomato sauce, saute minced garlic in a little oil, add tomato sauce, cumin, coriander, chili powder, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 15 minutes.
To assemble, layer rice, lentils, and macaroni in individual bowls. Top with chickpeas and crispy fried onions.

Serve hot with tomato sauce, a splash of vinegar, and hot sauce on the side. 7 Main Courses Mulukhiya A cherished Egyptian soup made from jute mallow leaves, creating a thick, viscous green broth that is deeply flavored with garlic, coriander, and tender meat. This iconic dish is a staple at every Egyptian family table. Calories: 320 kcal Serves: 6 people Time: 2 hours Ingredients 500g fresh or frozen jute mallow leaves (molokhia) 2 bay leaves 500g beef or lamb chunks with bone 1 cinnamon stick 6 cups beef or chicken broth 1/2 tsp black pepper 8 cloves garlic, minced Salt to taste 2 tbsp ground coriander Fresh lemon for serving 2 tbsp butter or ghee White rice for serving 1 large onion, quartered Baladi bread for serving Instructions
Boil the meat chunks with onion, bay leaves, cinnamon stick, salt, and pepper in 8 cups of water for 90 minutes until tender. Strain to make broth. Reserve the meat. 8 Main Courses
If using fresh molokhia leaves, wash thoroughly, remove stems, and finely chop or blend until finely minced. If frozen, thaw completely.
Bring 6 cups of broth to a gentle boil. Add the molokhia leaves and stir well. Simmer on low heat for 10 minutes. Do not overcook or over-stir.
In a small pan, melt butter. Add half the minced garlic and all the coriander. Saute until fragrant and golden (takleya).
Pour the garlic-coriander mixture over the molokhia and stir gently. Add the cooked meat.
In the same small pan, saute the remaining garlic in butter u

recipes.name: Mulukhiya
recipes.description: A cherished Egyptian soup made from jute mallow leaves, creating a thick, viscous green broth that is deeply flavored with garlic, coriander, and tender meat. This iconic dish is a staple at every Egyptian family table.
ingredients: 500g fresh or frozen jute mallow leaves (molokhia)
500g beef or lamb chunks with bone
instructions: Boil the meat chunks with onion, bay leaves, cinnamon stick, salt, and pepper in 8 cups of water for 90 minutes until tender. Strain to make broth. Reserve the meat. 8 Main Courses
If using fresh molokhia leaves, wash thoroughly, remove stems, and finely chop or blend until finely minced. If frozen, thaw completely.
Bring 6 cups of broth to a gentle boil. Add the molokhia leaves and stir well. Simmer on low heat for 10 minutes. Do not overcook or over-stir.
In a small pan, melt butter. Add half the minced garlic and all the coriander. Saute until fragrant and golden (takleya).
Pour the garlic-coriander mixture over the molokhia and stir gently. Add the cooked meat.
In the same small pan, saute the remaining garlic in butter until golden and add as a garnish on top.

Serve hot with white rice, fresh lemon wedges, and baladi bread. 9 Main Courses Mahshi Colorful stuffed vegetables filled with seasoned rice and fresh herbs, then slow-cooked in a rich tomato broth. A labor of love that brings together zucchini, eggplant, peppers, and grape leaves in one spectacular dish. Calories: 380 kcal Serves: 8 people Time: 2 hours 30 min Ingredients 4 medium zucchini, cored 1 large onion, finely chopped 2 small eggplants, cored 4 cloves garlic, minced 4 bell peppers (mixed colors), tops removed 1/2 cup tomato paste 1 jar grape leaves (about 50 leaves) 1/3 cup olive oil 2 cups Egyptian short-grain rice 1 tsp ground cumin 3 ripe tomatoes, diced 1/2 tsp allspice 1 bunch fresh dill, chopped 1/2 tsp cinnamon 1 bunch fresh parsley, chopped Juice of 2 lemons 1 bunch fresh coriander, chopped Salt and pepper to taste Instructions 10 Main Courses
Wash and core the zucchini, eggplant, and peppers. Soak grape leaves in warm water to remove brine.
Rinse the rice and mix with chopped tomatoes, fresh herbs, onion, garlic, olive oil, cumin, allspice, cinnamon, salt, and pepper.
Fill each vegetable about 3/4 full with the rice mixture. For grape leaves, place a small amount of filling and roll tightly.
Arrange the stuffed vegetables tightly in a large deep pot, starting with tomato slices on the bottom to prevent sticking.
Mix tomato paste with 4 cups of warm water and lemon juice. Pour over the vegetables until just cov

recipes.name: Mahshi
recipes.description: Colorful stuffed vegetables filled with seasoned rice and fresh herbs, then slow-cooked in a rich tomato broth. A labor of love that brings together zucchini, eggplant, peppers, and grape leaves in one spectacular dish.
instructions: Wash and core the zucchini, eggplant, and peppers. Soak grape leaves in warm water to remove brine.
Rinse the rice and mix with chopped tomatoes, fresh herbs, onion, garlic, olive oil, cumin, allspice, cinnamon, salt, and pepper.
Fill each vegetable about 3/4 full with the rice mixture. For grape leaves, place a small amount of filling and roll tightly.
Arrange the stuffed vegetables tightly in a large deep pot, starting with tomato slices on the bottom to prevent sticking.
Mix tomato paste with 4 cups of warm water and lemon juice. Pour over the vegetables until just covered.
Place a heat-proof plate on top to weigh down the mahshi during cooking. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered for 60-75 minutes.

Let rest for 15 minutes before serving. Serve warm with the cooking broth spooned over top. 11 Main Courses Fatta A festive celebratory dish featuring tender lamb, fragrant rice, and pieces of crispy toasted bread, all layered together and topped with a garlicky tomato sauce and white vinegar dressing. Traditionally served during Eid and special occasions. Calories: 650 kcal Serves: 6 people Time: 2 hours Ingredients 800g lamb chunks 2 bay leaves 2 cups Egyptian rice 1 cinnamon stick 4 pieces baladi bread or pita 2 cardamom pods 6 cloves garlic, minced 1/2 cup toasted nuts (almonds, pine nuts) 3 tbsp ghee or butter 1 tsp tomato paste 1/2 cup white vinegar 1/2 tsp ground cumin 1 cup beef broth Salt and pepper to taste 1 large onion, quartered Instructions 12 Main Courses
Boil the lamb with onion, bay leaves, cinnamon, cardamom, salt, and pepper until very tender, about 90 minutes. Reserve the broth.
Toast the bread in the oven until crispy and golden. Break into bite-sized pieces.
Cook the rice in 3 cups of the reserved lamb broth.
For the garlic sauce (takleya), saute 4 cloves of minced garlic in ghee until golden. Add tomato paste, cumin, and a ladle of broth. Simmer for 5 minutes.
For the vinegar dressing, saute the remaining 2 cloves garlic in ghee, then add white vinegar and heat through.
To assemble, place toasted bread pi

recipes.name: Fatta
recipes.description: A festive celebratory dish featuring tender lamb, fragrant rice, and pieces of crispy toasted bread, all layered together and topped with a garlicky tomato sauce and white vinegar dressing. Traditionally served during Eid and special occasions.
instructions: Boil the lamb with onion, bay leaves, cinnamon, cardamom, salt, and pepper until very tender, about 90 minutes. Reserve the broth.
Toast the bread in the oven until crispy and golden. Break into bite-sized pieces.
Cook the rice in 3 cups of the reserved lamb broth.
For the garlic sauce (takleya), saute 4 cloves of minced garlic in ghee until golden. Add tomato paste, cumin, and a ladle of broth. Simmer for 5 minutes.
For the vinegar dressing, saute the remaining 2 cloves garlic in ghee, then add white vinegar and heat through.
To assemble, place toasted bread pieces on the bottom of a serving dish. Top with cooked rice.
Arrange the tender lamb on top of the rice. Pour the garlic tomato sauce and vinegar dressing over everything.

Garnish with toasted nuts and serve immediately while hot. 13 Main Courses Kabab Halla A comforting slow-cooked beef stew with tender chunks of meat, potatoes, carrots, and onions simmered in a rich spiced gravy. This home-style dish is the definition of Egyptian comfort food, perfect for family dinners. Calories: 480 kcal Serves: 6 people Time: 2 hours 15 min Ingredients 800g beef chuck, cut into large chunks 1 tsp ground coriander 3 medium potatoes, peeled and quartered 1/2 tsp allspice 3 carrots, cut into chunks 1/2 tsp cinnamon 2 large onions, quartered 1 bay leaf 4 cloves garlic, minced 4 cups beef broth 3 tbsp vegetable oil Salt and black pepper to taste 2 tbsp tomato paste Fresh parsley for garnish 1 tsp ground cumin Instructions 14 Main Courses
Season the beef chunks with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a large pot and sear the meat on all sides until browned. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, saute the onions until golden. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato paste, cumin, coriander, allspice, and cinnamon. Stir well and cook for 2 minutes.
Return the beef to the pot. Add beef broth and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 60 minutes.
Add the potatoes and carrots. Continue cooking for another 30-40 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. The gravy should be thick and rich.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot with white rice or baladi brea

recipes.name: Kabab Halla
recipes.description: A comforting slow-cooked beef stew with tender chunks of meat, potatoes, carrots, and onions simmered in a rich spiced gravy. This home-style dish is the definition of Egyptian comfort food, perfect for family dinners.
ingredients: 800g beef chuck, cut into large chunks
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season the beef chunks with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a large pot and sear the meat on all sides until browned. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, saute the onions until golden. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato paste, cumin, coriander, allspice, and cinnamon. Stir well and cook for 2 minutes.
Return the beef to the pot. Add beef broth and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 60 minutes.
Add the potatoes and carrots. Continue cooking for another 30-40 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. The gravy should be thick and rich.

Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot with white rice or baladi bread. 15 Main Courses Sayadiyah A coastal specialty featuring seasoned fried fish served on a bed of spiced rice with caramelized onions and toasted nuts. This aromatic dish brings the flavors of the Mediterranean to your table with its perfect balance of spice and seafood. Calories: 450 kcal Serves: 4 people Time: 50 minutes Ingredients 500g firm white fish fillets (sea bass or red mullet) 1/4 tsp cardamom 2 cups basmati rice 2 bay leaves 3 large onions, thinly sliced 3 cups fish stock 1/4 cup toasted pine nuts 3 tbsp olive oil 1/4 cup toasted almonds 2 tbsp all-purpose flour 1 tsp cumin 1 lemon, juiced 1/2 tsp turmeric Salt and pepper to taste 1/2 tsp cinnamon Instructions 16 Main Courses
Season fish fillets with salt, pepper, and half the cumin. Dredge lightly in flour and pan-fry in olive oil until golden. Set aside.
Caramelize the sliced onions in oil over medium-low heat until deep golden brown, about 20 minutes. Remove half for garnish.
Add remaining cumin, turmeric, cinnamon, and cardamom to the onions in the pot. Stir for 1 minute.
Add rinsed basmati rice and stir to coat with spices. Add fish stock and bay leaves. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 18-20 minutes until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Fluff the rice and transfer to a serving platter. Arrange the fried fish on top.
Garnish with caramelized onions, toasted pine nuts, and almonds. Drizzle with lemon juice and serve. 17 Main Courses Bamia A beloved okra stew where tend

recipes.name: Sayadiyah
recipes.description: A coastal specialty featuring seasoned fried fish served on a bed of spiced rice with caramelized onions and toasted nuts. This aromatic dish brings the flavors of the Mediterranean to your table with its perfect balance of spice and seafood.
ingredients: 500g firm white fish fillets (sea bass or red mullet)
instructions: Season fish fillets with salt, pepper, and half the cumin. Dredge lightly in flour and pan-fry in olive oil until golden. Set aside.
Caramelize the sliced onions in oil over medium-low heat until deep golden brown, about 20 minutes. Remove half for garnish.
Add remaining cumin, turmeric, cinnamon, and cardamom to the onions in the pot. Stir for 1 minute.
Add rinsed basmati rice and stir to coat with spices. Add fish stock and bay leaves. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 18-20 minutes until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Fluff the rice and transfer to a serving platter. Arrange the fried fish on top.

Garnish with caramelized onions, toasted pine nuts, and almonds. Drizzle with lemon juice and serve. 17 Main Courses Bamia A beloved okra stew where tender lamb simmers with fresh okra in a rich tomato sauce, creating a thick, flavorful dish. The okra acts as a natural thickener while adding its distinctive earthy flavor to this comforting Egyptian classic. Calories: 350 kcal Serves: 6 people Time: 1 hour 45 min Ingredients 500g lamb or beef chunks 2 cups beef broth 500g fresh okra, stems trimmed 1 tsp ground coriander 400g diced tomatoes 1/2 tsp ground cumin 2 tbsp tomato paste 1/2 tsp cinnamon 2 medium onions, chopped 1 tbsp dried mint 4 cloves garlic, minced 1 lemon, juiced 2 tbsp olive oil Salt and pepper to taste Instructions
Saute onions in olive oil until golden. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute. 18 Main Courses
Add meat chunks and brown on all sides. Add cumin, coriander, cinnamon, salt, and pepper.
Add diced tomatoes, tomato paste, and beef broth. Bring to a boil, then simmer covered for 60 minutes until meat is tender.
Add the okra and dried mint. Simmer for another 20-25 minutes until okra is tender.
Stir in lemon juice and adjust seasoning. The sauce should be thick.

Serve hot with white rice and baladi bread. 19 Main Courses Molokhia with Rabbit The traditional way to enjoy molokhia - with tender rabbit meat that imparts a delicate, sweet flavor to the broth. This pairing is considered the ultimate way to serve Egypt's most iconic green soup. Calories: 290 kcal Serves: 6 people Time: 2 hours 15 min Ingredients 1

recipes.name: Macarona Bechamel
recipes.description: Egypt's answer to lasagna - layers of penne pasta, spiced minced meat, and creamy bechamel sauce baked to golden perfection. This beloved casserole is a staple at family gatherings and the ultimate comfort food.
instructions: Cook the penne pasta until al dente. Drain, toss with a little butter, and set aside. 34 Main Courses
For the meat layer: saute onion until golden. Add garlic and ground meat. Brown well, breaking up lumps. Add tomato paste, cinnamon, allspice, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 15 minutes.
For the bechamel: melt butter in a saucepan. Add flour and whisk for 2 minutes. Gradually add warm milk, whisking constantly until smooth and thickened.
Season bechamel with nutmeg and salt. Remove from heat and stir in beaten eggs quickly to prevent scrambling.
Preheat oven to 180C (350F). Grease a large baking dish.
Layer half the pasta, then all the meat mixture, then remaining pasta. Pour bechamel evenly over the top.

Sprinkle grated cheese on top. Bake for 40-45 minutes until golden and bubbling. Let rest 15 minutes before cutting. 35 Main Courses Sogo2 A spicy Egyptian sausage sandwich featuring traditional beef sausages seasoned with garlic, coriander, and chili, pan-fried and stuffed into soft baladi bread with tahini and pickled vegetables. A beloved street food classic. Calories: 450 kcal Serves: 4 people Time: 25 minutes Ingredients 500g Egyptian beef sausages (sogo2) or merguez 2 tbsp olive oil 4 baladi bread rolls or pita pockets 1 tsp ground cumin 1 large onion, sliced 1/2 tsp paprika 2 tomatoes, sliced Fresh parsley, chopped 1/2 cup pickled vegetables (torshi) Salt and pepper to taste 1/4 cup tahini sauce Instructions
If using whole sausages, slice them diagonally into 1cm thick pieces. If using loose sausage meat, form into small patties. 36 Main Courses
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Fry the sausages with sliced onion until browned and crispy, about 8-10 minutes.
Season with cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook for another 2 minutes.
Warm the bread rolls. Split open and spread tahini sauce inside.
Fill each roll with the fried sausage and onion mixture.

Top with sliced tomatoes, pickled vegetables, and fresh parsley. Serve immediately while hot. 37 Main Courses Waraket Lahma Tender seasoned beef and vegetables wrapped in foil and baked until perfectly cooked, creating individual parcels of juicy, flavorful meat that makes for an impressive presentation at any dinner table. Calories: 420 kcal Serves:

recipes.name: Baba Ghanoush
recipes.description: A smoky, creamy roasted eggplant dip blended with tahini, lemon, and garlic. The charred skin of the eggplant infuses the dip with an irresistible smoky depth that makes this one of the most beloved mezze dishes across Egypt.
ingredients: 2 tbsp olive oil, plus more for drizzling
instructions: Prick the eggplants with a fork. Roast over an open flame, under a broiler, or on a grill, turning occasionally, until the skin is charred and the flesh is soft (15-20 minutes).
Let the eggplants cool slightly. Peel off the charred skin and discard. 47 Appetizers
Place the roasted flesh in a colander and let drain for 10 minutes to remove excess liquid.
Mash the eggplant flesh with a fork, leaving some texture, or pulse briefly in a food processor.
Mix in tahini, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, cumin, and salt. Stir well to combine.
Transfer to a serving bowl. Create a swirl pattern on top with the back of a spoon.

Drizzle with olive oil and pomegranate molasses. Garnish with parsley and sumac. Serve with warm pita bread. 48 Appetizers Hummus The iconic creamy chickpea dip that has conquered the world. Smooth, rich, and deeply satisfying, this Egyptian version balances tahini, lemon, and garlic in perfect harmony for the ultimate mezze centerpiece. Calories: 180 kcal Serves: 6 people Time: 15 minutes Ingredients 2 cups cooked chickpeas (or 1 can, drained) 1/2 tsp ground cumin 1/3 cup tahini paste 1/4 tsp ground paprika 3 tbsp lemon juice Salt to taste 2 cloves garlic Whole chickpeas for garnish 3 tbsp ice water Fresh parsley for garnish 2 tbsp olive oil, plus more for drizzling Warm pita bread for serving Instructions
If using canned chickpeas, rinse well. For extra smooth hummus, peel the chickpeas by gently pinching each one.
In a food processor, blend chickpeas until they form a coarse paste, about 1 minute. 49 Appetizers
Add tahini, lemon juice, garlic, and ice water. Blend for 2-3 minutes until very smooth and creamy. The ice water helps achieve a lighter texture.
With the processor running, drizzle in olive oil and add cumin and salt. Blend until fully incorporated.
Taste and adjust seasoning - add more lemon, salt, or tahini as needed. The hummus should be smooth, creamy, and well-balanced.
Spread on a shallow plate, creating a well in the center with the back of a spoon.

Drizzle generously with olive oil, sprinkle with paprika, and garnish with whole chickpeas and parsley. Serve with warm pita bread. 50 Appetizers Tahini Sauce A versatile sesame seed sauce that accompanies countless Egyptian dishes. Creamy, nutty, and tangy, this sauce trans

recipes.name: Hummus
recipes.description: The iconic creamy chickpea dip that has conquered the world. Smooth, rich, and deeply satisfying, this Egyptian version balances tahini, lemon, and garlic in perfect harmony for the ultimate mezze centerpiece.
ingredients: 2 tbsp olive oil, plus more for drizzling
instructions: If using canned chickpeas, rinse well. For extra smooth hummus, peel the chickpeas by gently pinching each one.
In a food processor, blend chickpeas until they form a coarse paste, about 1 minute. 49 Appetizers
Add tahini, lemon juice, garlic, and ice water. Blend for 2-3 minutes until very smooth and creamy. The ice water helps achieve a lighter texture.
With the processor running, drizzle in olive oil and add cumin and salt. Blend until fully incorporated.
Taste and adjust seasoning - add more lemon, salt, or tahini as needed. The hummus should be smooth, creamy, and well-balanced.
Spread on a shallow plate, creating a well in the center with the back of a spoon.

Drizzle generously with olive oil, sprinkle with paprika, and garnish with whole chickpeas and parsley. Serve with warm pita bread. 50 Appetizers Tahini Sauce A versatile sesame seed sauce that accompanies countless Egyptian dishes. Creamy, nutty, and tangy, this sauce transforms falafel, grilled meats, and vegetables into something truly special with minimal effort. Calories: 90 kcal Serves: 8 people Time: 10 minutes Ingredients 1/2 cup tahini paste 1 tbsp olive oil 3 tbsp lemon juice 1/2 tsp ground cumin 2 cloves garlic, minced Pinch of salt 1/4 cup cold water (adjust as needed) Fresh parsley, chopped (optional) Instructions
In a bowl, whisk together tahini paste, lemon juice, and minced garlic. The mixture will initially thicken and seize up.
Add cold water one tablespoon at a time, whisking continuously, until the sauce reaches a smooth, pourable consistency.
Stir in olive oil and cumin. Season with salt to taste. 51 Appetizers
Taste and adjust - add more lemon for tanginess, more water for thinner sauce, or more tahini for thickness.
Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with chopped parsley if desired.

Serve as a dip, dressing, or sauce alongside falafel, grilled meats, or vegetables. 52 Appetizers Torshi A vibrant assortment of pickled vegetables in a tangy, aromatic brine. These colorful crunchy pickles are an essential accompaniment to Egyptian meals, cutting through rich dishes with their sharp, refreshing acidity. Calories: 35 kcal Serves: 10 people Time: 3 days (pickling) Ingredients 2 medium turnips, peeled and sliced 4 cups water 2 carrots, peeled and sliced 1/2 cup

recipes.name: Tahini Sauce
recipes.description: A versatile sesame seed sauce that accompanies countless Egyptian dishes. Creamy, nutty, and tangy, this sauce transforms falafel, grilled meats, and vegetables into something truly special with minimal effort.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together tahini paste, lemon juice, and minced garlic. The mixture will initially thicken and seize up.
Add cold water one tablespoon at a time, whisking continuously, until the sauce reaches a smooth, pourable consistency.
Stir in olive oil and cumin. Season with salt to taste. 51 Appetizers
Taste and adjust - add more lemon for tanginess, more water for thinner sauce, or more tahini for thickness.
Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with chopped parsley if desired.

Serve as a dip, dressing, or sauce alongside falafel, grilled meats, or vegetables. 52 Appetizers Torshi A vibrant assortment of pickled vegetables in a tangy, aromatic brine. These colorful crunchy pickles are an essential accompaniment to Egyptian meals, cutting through rich dishes with their sharp, refreshing acidity. Calories: 35 kcal Serves: 10 people Time: 3 days (pickling) Ingredients 2 medium turnips, peeled and sliced 4 cups water 2 carrots, peeled and sliced 1/2 cup white vinegar 1 small cauliflower, broken into florets 3 tbsp coarse salt 2 cucumbers, sliced 1 tsp sugar 2 small beets, peeled and sliced (for color) 1 tbsp pickling spice (coriander, mustard seeds, peppercorns) 4 cloves garlic, peeled Fresh dill sprigs 2 fresh chili peppers (optional) Instructions
Wash and prepare all vegetables. Pack them tightly into clean, sterilized glass jars along with garlic cloves, chili peppers, and fresh dill. 53 Appetizers
In a saucepan, bring water, vinegar, salt, sugar, and pickling spices to a boil. Stir until salt dissolves completely.
Pour the hot brine over the vegetables, ensuring they are completely submerged. Leave 1cm headspace.
Seal the jars tightly and let cool to room temperature.
Store in a cool, dark place for at least 3 days before eating. The longer they pickle, the better the flavor.
Once opened, refrigerate. The torshi will keep for several weeks.

Serve alongside main dishes, sandwiches, or as part of a mezze spread. 54 Appetizers Salata Baladi The quintessential Egyptian salad of finely diced tomatoes, cucumbers, and onions tossed with fresh herbs and a bright lemon dressing. Simple, refreshing, and the perfect companion to any heavy Egyptian main course. Calories: 60 kcal Serves: 4 people Time: 15 minutes Ingredients 4 ripe tomatoes, finely diced 2 tbsp fresh mint, chopped 2 cucumbers, finely diced 3 tbsp olive oil 1 small red onion, fine

recipes.name: Salata Baladi
recipes.description: The quintessential Egyptian salad of finely diced tomatoes, cucumbers, and onions tossed with fresh herbs and a bright lemon dressing. Simple, refreshing, and the perfect companion to any heavy Egyptian main course.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Dice the tomatoes, cucumbers, and onion into small, uniform pieces (about 5mm). This fine dice is characteristic of baladi salad.
Combine all diced vegetables in a serving bowl. 55 Appetizers
Add chopped parsley and mint. Toss to combine.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, sumac, salt, and pepper.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine.
Taste and adjust seasoning. The salad should be bright and well-seasoned.

Serve immediately as an accompaniment to main dishes, or chill briefly for a more refreshing salad. 56 Appetizers Beetroot Salad Vibrant roasted beets tossed with a zesty garliclemon dressing and fresh herbs. This stunning magenta salad adds a burst of color and earthy sweetness to any Egyptian spread, with a tangy kick that awakens the palate. Calories: 80 kcal Serves: 4 people Time: 1 hour (mostly roasting) Ingredients 4 medium beetroots, washed and trimmed 2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped 3 cloves garlic, minced 1 tbsp fresh dill, chopped 3 tbsp olive oil 1/2 tsp ground cumin 2 tbsp lemon juice Salt and black pepper to taste 1 tbsp red wine vinegar Feta cheese crumbles (optional) 1/2 small red onion, thinly sliced Instructions
Preheat oven to 200C (400F). Wrap each beetroot individually in foil and place on a baking sheet.
Roast for 45-60 minutes until tender when pierced with a knife. Let cool completely. 57 Appetizers
Once cool, peel the beetroots by rubbing the skins off with your fingers or a paper towel. They should slip off easily.
Cut the beetroots into bite-sized cubes or wedges.
In a bowl, whisk together garlic, olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Toss the beetroots and red onion with the dressing. Let marinate for at least 15 minutes.

Garnish with fresh parsley and dill. Add feta cheese if desired. Serve chilled or at room temperature. 58 Appetizers Eggplant with Vinegar Crispy fried eggplant slices marinated in a pungent garlic-vinegar dressing with a hint of chili. This addictive appetizer balances the richness of fried eggplant with sharp, tangy flavors for a truly memorable mezze dish. Calories: 150 kcal Serves: 4 people Time: 30 minutes Ingredients 2 large eggplants, sliced into 1cm rounds 1/2 tsp ground cumin 3 cloves garlic, minced Salt to taste 1/4 cup whi

recipes.name: Fava Beans Salad
recipes.description: A refreshing and protein-packed salad of tender fava beans tossed with tomatoes, herbs, and a bright lemon dressing. This nutritious dish can serve as a light meal or hearty appetizer, showcasing the ancient Egyptian staple legume.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked fava beans (or 1 can, drained)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: If using dried fava beans, soak overnight and cook until tender. If using canned, rinse well under cold water.
Combine fava beans, diced tomatoes, red onion, parsley, and mint in a serving bowl. 61 Appetizers
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine.
Taste and adjust seasoning. The salad should be bright and well-seasoned.
Arrange hard-boiled egg quarters on top of the salad.

Serve with warm baladi bread. Can be served at room temperature or slightly chilled. 62 Appetizers Warak Enab Delicate grape leaves stuffed with seasoned rice and fresh herbs, rolled into perfect little parcels and simmered until tender. These bite-sized gems are the crown jewel of any Egyptian mezze spread, offering a burst of flavor in every mouthful. Calories: 45 kcal per piece Serves: 6 people Time: 1 hour 30 min Ingredients 1 jar grape leaves (about 50 leaves) 3 cloves garlic, minced 1 cup short-grain rice 1/3 cup olive oil 1 medium tomato, finely diced Juice of 2 lemons 1/2 cup fresh parsley, finely chopped 1 tsp ground allspice 1/2 cup fresh dill, finely chopped 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon 1/2 cup fresh mint, finely chopped Salt and pepper to taste 1 small onion, finely minced Instructions
Rinse grape leaves under warm water to remove brine. Soak in hot water for 10 minutes to soften. 63 Appetizers
Drain and pat dry. Rinse rice and mix with diced tomato, fresh herbs, onion, garlic, 2 tablespoons olive oil, allspice, cinnamon, salt, and pepper.
Place a grape leaf vein-side up on a work surface. Place 1 teaspoon of filling near the stem end. Fold in the sides and roll tightly.
Line the bottom of a heavy pot with torn or damaged grape leaves. Arrange the rolled leaves seamside down in tight layers.
Place a heat-proof plate on top to weigh down the leaves. Add enough hot water to just cover, plus lemon juice and remaining olive oil.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 45-50 minutes until rice is tender.
Let cool to room temperature. Serve with additional lemon wedges. 64 Appetizers Falafel (Taameya) The iconic Egyptian fava bean falafel, known for its distinctive bright

recipes.name: Warak Enab
recipes.description: Delicate grape leaves stuffed with seasoned rice and fresh herbs, rolled into perfect little parcels and simmered until tender. These bite-sized gems are the crown jewel of any Egyptian mezze spread, offering a burst of flavor in every mouthful.
instructions: Rinse grape leaves under warm water to remove brine. Soak in hot water for 10 minutes to soften. 63 Appetizers
Drain and pat dry. Rinse rice and mix with diced tomato, fresh herbs, onion, garlic, 2 tablespoons olive oil, allspice, cinnamon, salt, and pepper.
Place a grape leaf vein-side up on a work surface. Place 1 teaspoon of filling near the stem end. Fold in the sides and roll tightly.
Line the bottom of a heavy pot with torn or damaged grape leaves. Arrange the rolled leaves seamside down in tight layers.
Place a heat-proof plate on top to weigh down the leaves. Add enough hot water to just cover, plus lemon juice and remaining olive oil.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 45-50 minutes until rice is tender.

Let cool to room temperature. Serve with additional lemon wedges. 64 Appetizers Falafel (Taameya) The iconic Egyptian fava bean falafel, known for its distinctive bright green interior from fresh herbs. Crispy on the outside, tender and fragrant within, these little patties are Egypt's most famous street food export and a vegetarian protein powerhouse. Calories: 65 kcal per piece Serves: 6 people Time: 45 minutes Ingredients 2 cups dried split fava beans, skinless 1/2 tsp cayenne pepper 1 large bunch fresh coriander (2 cups chopped) 1 tsp baking powder 1/2 bunch fresh parsley (1 cup chopped) 1 tbsp sesame seeds 1 medium onion, quartered 1 tsp salt 6 cloves garlic 1/2 tsp black pepper 1 tsp ground cumin Vegetable oil for deep frying 1 tsp ground coriander Tahini sauce for serving Instructions
Soak split fava beans in cold water overnight. Drain well. 65 Appetizers
In a food processor, coarsely grind the drained fava beans with coriander, parsley, onion, and garlic.
Add cumin, ground coriander, cayenne, salt, and pepper. Pulse to combine. The mixture should hold together when pressed.
Stir in baking powder and let the mixture rest for 10 minutes.
Heat oil to 180C (350F). Wet your hands and shape the mixture into small patties or quenelle shapes. Press sesame seeds on top.
Fry in batches for 3-4 minutes until deep golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
Serve hot with tahini sauce, fresh sal

recipes.name: Lentil Soup
recipes.description: Ads) A velvety, warming soup of red lentils seasoned with cumin and finished with a squeeze of lemon and crispy fried onions. This nourishing, proteinrich soup is a comforting staple that has sustained Egyptians through countless winters.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Saute onion and carrots until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic, cumin, turmeric, and coriander. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add red lentils and broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 25-30 minutes until lentils are very soft.
Remove bay leaf. Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth. Alternatively, blend in batches.
Return to heat and stir in lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper.
If too thick, thin with a little water or broth to reach desired consistency.

Serve hot, garnished with crispy fried onions, a drizzle of olive oil, and lemon wedges. Serve with warm pita bread. 76 Desserts PA R T T H R E E Desserts Sweet indulgences from the land of the pharaohs, where honey, nuts, and pastry reign supreme 77 Desserts Baklava Lavish layers of gossamer-thin phyllo pastry interleaved with chopped nuts and bathed in fragrant syrup. This iconic dessert represents the pinnacle of Middle Eastern pastry craft, each bite a perfect harmony of crunch, sweetness, and aromatic spice. Calories: 280 kcal per piece Serves: 24 pieces Time: 1 hour 30 min Ingredients 500g phyllo pastry sheets For the syrup: 2 cups sugar 300g mixed nuts (walnuts, pistachios, almonds), 1 cup water finely chopped 1/2 cup honey 200g unsalted butter, melted 1 tbsp lemon juice 2 tsp ground cinnamon 1 tsp rose water 1/2 tsp ground cloves 1 tsp orange blossom water Instructions
Make the syrup: combine sugar, water, honey, and lemon juice in a saucepan. Boil for 10 minutes until slightly thickened. Add rose water and orange blossom water. Cool completely. 78 Desserts
Mix chopped nuts with cinnamon and cloves. Grease a 33x23cm baking dish with melted butter.
Place 8 sheets of phyllo in the dish, brushing each with melted butter. Keep remaining phyllo covered with a damp towel.
Spread a third of the nut mixture evenly over the phyllo. Layer 5 more buttered phyllo sheets on top.
Repeat with another layer of nuts and phyllo, then a final layer of nuts. Top with 8-10 sheets of buttered phyllo.
Using a sharp knife, cut the baklava into diamond shapes, cutting through all layers. Pour any remaining butter over the

recipes.name: Baklava
recipes.description: Lavish layers of gossamer-thin phyllo pastry interleaved with chopped nuts and bathed in fragrant syrup. This iconic dessert represents the pinnacle of Middle Eastern pastry craft, each bite a perfect harmony of crunch, sweetness, and aromatic spice.
instructions: Make the syrup: combine sugar, water, honey, and lemon juice in a saucepan. Boil for 10 minutes until slightly thickened. Add rose water and orange blossom water. Cool completely. 78 Desserts
Mix chopped nuts with cinnamon and cloves. Grease a 33x23cm baking dish with melted butter.
Place 8 sheets of phyllo in the dish, brushing each with melted butter. Keep remaining phyllo covered with a damp towel.
Spread a third of the nut mixture evenly over the phyllo. Layer 5 more buttered phyllo sheets on top.
Repeat with another layer of nuts and phyllo, then a final layer of nuts. Top with 8-10 sheets of buttered phyllo.
Using a sharp knife, cut the baklava into diamond shapes, cutting through all layers. Pour any remaining butter over the top.

Bake at 180C (350F) for 45-50 minutes until deep golden and crisp. Immediately pour cooled syrup over the hot baklava. Let rest for at least 4 hours before serving. 79 Desserts Basbousa A beloved semolina cake soaked in fragrant syrup, with a tender, grainy texture and a golden, slightly crisp top. Each diamond-shaped piece is crowned with a toasted almond, making this the most iconic Egyptian dessert after any family meal. Calories: 220 kcal per piece Serves: 16 pieces Time: 1 hour Ingredients 2 cups fine semolina 1 tsp vanilla extract 1/2 cup all-purpose flour 16 whole blanched almonds 1/2 cup sugar For the syrup: 2 cups sugar 1/2 cup unsalted butter, melted 1 1/2 cups water 1/2 cup plain yogurt 1 tbsp lemon juice 1/2 cup milk 1 tsp rose water 1 tsp baking powder Instructions
Make the syrup: combine sugar, water, and lemon juice. Boil for 10 minutes. Add rose water and set 80 Desserts
Preheat oven to 180C (350F). Grease a 33x23cm baking dish.
In a large bowl, mix semolina, flour, sugar, and baking powder.
Add melted butter, yogurt, milk, and vanilla. Mix well to form a thick batter.
Pour into the prepared dish and spread evenly with a spatula. Let rest for 15 minutes.
Score the batter into diamond shapes with a knife. Place one almond in the center of each diamond.
Bake for 30-35 minutes until golden. Immediately pour cooled syrup over the hot basbousa. Let soak for at least 2 hours before serving. 81 Desserts Kunafa Shredded phyllo pastry encasi

recipes.name: Basbousa
recipes.description: A beloved semolina cake soaked in fragrant syrup, with a tender, grainy texture and a golden, slightly crisp top. Each diamond-shaped piece is crowned with a toasted almond, making this the most iconic Egyptian dessert after any family meal.
instructions: Make the syrup: combine sugar, water, and lemon juice. Boil for 10 minutes. Add rose water and set 80 Desserts
Preheat oven to 180C (350F). Grease a 33x23cm baking dish.
In a large bowl, mix semolina, flour, sugar, and baking powder.
Add melted butter, yogurt, milk, and vanilla. Mix well to form a thick batter.
Pour into the prepared dish and spread evenly with a spatula. Let rest for 15 minutes.
Score the batter into diamond shapes with a knife. Place one almond in the center of each diamond.

Bake for 30-35 minutes until golden. Immediately pour cooled syrup over the hot basbousa. Let soak for at least 2 hours before serving. 81 Desserts Kunafa Shredded phyllo pastry encasing a layer of sweet melted cheese, baked until crispy and golden, then drenched in fragrant syrup. This dramatic dessert is a showstopper at Egyptian celebrations, combining crunch, creaminess, and sweetness in every bite. Calories: 350 kcal per piece Serves: 12 pieces Time: 1 hour Ingredients 500g kunafa dough (shredded phyllo) For the syrup: 2 cups sugar 300g mozzarella cheese, shredded 1 cup water 200g ricotta cheese 1 tbsp lemon juice 200g unsalted butter, melted 1 tsp rose water 2 tbsp orange food coloring (optional) 1 tsp orange blossom water 1/2 cup crushed pistachios for garnish Instructions
Make the syrup: combine sugar, water, and lemon juice. Boil for 10 minutes. Add rose and orange blossom water. Cool completely. 82 Desserts
Preheat oven to 180C (350F). Grease a 30cm round baking dish.
Shred the kunafa dough into shorter strands using your hands. Toss with melted butter (and food coloring if using) until evenly coated.
Press half the kunafa mixture firmly into the bottom of the dish, creating a compact base.
Mix mozzarella and ricotta. Spread the cheese mixture evenly over the kunafa base.
Cover with the remaining kunafa, pressing down gently to seal the edges.

Bake for 40-45 minutes until the top is deep golden and crispy. Immediately flip onto a serving plate and pour cooled syrup over the entire surface. Garnish with crushed pistachios and serve warm. 83 Desserts Umm Ali Egypt's beloved bread pudding, named "Ali's Mother," featuring layers of flaky pastry soaked in sweetened milk with coconut, raisins, and toasted n

recipes.name: Kunafa
recipes.description: Shredded phyllo pastry encasing a layer of sweet melted cheese, baked until crispy and golden, then drenched in fragrant syrup. This dramatic dessert is a showstopper at Egyptian celebrations, combining crunch, creaminess, and sweetness in every bite.
instructions: Make the syrup: combine sugar, water, and lemon juice. Boil for 10 minutes. Add rose and orange blossom water. Cool completely. 82 Desserts
Preheat oven to 180C (350F). Grease a 30cm round baking dish.
Shred the kunafa dough into shorter strands using your hands. Toss with melted butter (and food coloring if using) until evenly coated.
Press half the kunafa mixture firmly into the bottom of the dish, creating a compact base.
Mix mozzarella and ricotta. Spread the cheese mixture evenly over the kunafa base.
Cover with the remaining kunafa, pressing down gently to seal the edges.

Bake for 40-45 minutes until the top is deep golden and crispy. Immediately flip onto a serving plate and pour cooled syrup over the entire surface. Garnish with crushed pistachios and serve warm. 83 Desserts Umm Ali Egypt's beloved bread pudding, named "Ali's Mother," featuring layers of flaky pastry soaked in sweetened milk with coconut, raisins, and toasted nuts. Baked until golden and bubbling, this warm, comforting dessert is pure indulgence. Calories: 380 kcal per serving Serves: 8 people Time: 45 minutes Ingredients 500g puff pastry sheets, baked until golden 1/4 cup raisins 4 cups whole milk 1/4 cup shredded coconut 1 cup heavy cream 1 tsp vanilla extract 3/4 cup sugar 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon 1/2 cup mixed nuts (almonds, pistachios, cashews), 2 tbsp butter, cut into small pieces toasted Instructions
Bake the puff pastry according to package instructions until golden and crispy. Let cool, then break into bite-sized pieces.
Preheat oven to 200C (400F). Grease a large baking dish. 84 Desserts
Spread half the pastry pieces in the baking dish. Sprinkle with half the nuts, raisins, and coconut.
Layer the remaining pastry pieces on top. Sprinkle with the remaining nuts, raisins, and coconut.
In a saucepan, heat milk, cream, and sugar until the sugar dissolves. Do not boil. Add vanilla extract.
Pour the hot milk mixture evenly over the pastry. Let it soak for 10 minutes. Press down gently with a spoon.

Dot the surface with butter pieces. Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden and bubbling. Serve warm, sprinkled with cinnamon. 85 Desserts Mahalabiya A silky, fragrant milk pudding delicately scented with rose water and oran

recipes.name: Umm Ali
recipes.description: Egypt's beloved bread pudding, named "Ali's Mother," featuring layers of flaky pastry soaked in sweetened milk with coconut, raisins, and toasted nuts. Baked until golden and bubbling, this warm, comforting dessert is pure indulgence.
ingredients: 500g puff pastry sheets, baked until golden
2 tbsp butter, cut into small pieces
instructions: Bake the puff pastry according to package instructions until golden and crispy. Let cool, then break into bite-sized pieces.
Preheat oven to 200C (400F). Grease a large baking dish. 84 Desserts
Spread half the pastry pieces in the baking dish. Sprinkle with half the nuts, raisins, and coconut.
Layer the remaining pastry pieces on top. Sprinkle with the remaining nuts, raisins, and coconut.
In a saucepan, heat milk, cream, and sugar until the sugar dissolves. Do not boil. Add vanilla extract.
Pour the hot milk mixture evenly over the pastry. Let it soak for 10 minutes. Press down gently with a spoon.

Dot the surface with butter pieces. Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden and bubbling. Serve warm, sprinkled with cinnamon. 85 Desserts Mahalabiya A silky, fragrant milk pudding delicately scented with rose water and orange blossom, set to a perfect wobble and garnished with crushed pistachios. This elegant dessert has graced Egyptian tables since the time of the pharaohs, offering a light, refined end to any meal. Calories: 150 kcal per serving Serves: 6 people Time: 20 minutes + chilling Ingredients 4 cups whole milk 1 tbsp orange blossom water 1/2 cup sugar 1/4 cup crushed pistachios for garnish 4 tbsp cornstarch Dried rose petals for garnish (optional) 2 tbsp rose water Ground cinnamon for garnish Instructions
Reserve 1/2 cup of cold milk and mix with cornstarch until smooth.
Heat the remaining milk with sugar in a saucepan, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
When the milk is hot (not boiling), stir in the cornstarch mixture. Whisk constantly. 86 Desserts
Continue cooking and stirring until the mixture thickens to a custard-like consistency, about 5-7 minutes. It should coat the back of a spoon.
Remove from heat and stir in rose water and orange blossom water.
Pour into individual serving glasses or bowls. Let cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 3 hours until set.

Before serving, garnish with crushed pistachios, rose petals, and a light dusting of cinnamon. 87 Desserts Roz Bel Laban (Dessert) The dessert version of Egypt's beloved rice pudding, creamy and comforting with fragrant vanilla and a dusting of cinnamon. Each spoonful delivers warm, nostalgic flavors that have made this a household favorite for gene

recipes.name: Mahalabiya
recipes.description: A silky, fragrant milk pudding delicately scented with rose water and orange blossom, set to a perfect wobble and garnished with crushed pistachios. This elegant dessert has graced Egyptian tables since the time of the pharaohs, offering a light, refined end to any meal.
instructions: Reserve 1/2 cup of cold milk and mix with cornstarch until smooth.
Heat the remaining milk with sugar in a saucepan, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
When the milk is hot (not boiling), stir in the cornstarch mixture. Whisk constantly. 86 Desserts
Continue cooking and stirring until the mixture thickens to a custard-like consistency, about 5-7 minutes. It should coat the back of a spoon.
Remove from heat and stir in rose water and orange blossom water.
Pour into individual serving glasses or bowls. Let cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 3 hours until set.

Before serving, garnish with crushed pistachios, rose petals, and a light dusting of cinnamon. 87 Desserts Roz Bel Laban (Dessert) The dessert version of Egypt's beloved rice pudding, creamy and comforting with fragrant vanilla and a dusting of cinnamon. Each spoonful delivers warm, nostalgic flavors that have made this a household favorite for generations of Egyptian families. Calories: 200 kcal per serving Serves: 6 people Time: 45 minutes Ingredients 1/2 cup Egyptian short-grain rice 1/2 tsp rose water 4 cups whole milk Ground cinnamon for dusting 1/2 cup sugar 2 tbsp crushed pistachios for garnish 1/4 cup heavy cream 2 tbsp raisins (optional) 1 tsp vanilla extract Instructions
Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear. 88 Desserts
In a saucepan, combine rice with 1 cup of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes until partially cooked.
Add milk and sugar. Stir well and bring to a gentle simmer over low heat.
Cook for 25-30 minutes, stirring frequently to prevent sticking, until the rice is very tender and the mixture has thickened to a creamy consistency.
Stir in heavy cream, vanilla extract, and rose water. Cook for another 2 minutes.
Remove from heat. The pudding will thicken further as it cools. Pour into serving bowls.

Serve warm or chilled, dusted generously with cinnamon and garnished with crushed pistachios and raisins. 89 Desserts Qatayef Half-moon stuffed pancakes that are a Ramadan tradition across Egypt. These sweet parcels are filled with cheese or nuts, then fried or baked until golden and soaked in syrup. The soft, yeasted batter creates a uniquely tender pancake that is irresistible. Calories: 180 kcal per piece S

recipes.name: Kahk
recipes.description: Ghorayebah Zalabia Sweet Goulash Balah El Sham Halawet El Jibn Aish El Saraya Couscous Dessert Saffron Rice Pudding Date-filled Cookies Fark El Saneen Muhallabia with Nuts Mucaddam Main Courses PA R T O N E Main Courses Hearty dishes that form the centerpiece of Egyptian family gatherings and celebrations 5 Main Courses Koshari Egypt's national dish and most beloved street food. Layers of rice, lentils, macaroni, and chickpeas topped with crispy fried onions and zesty tomato sauce create a hearty, satisfying vegetarian meal that has been enjoyed for generations.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Rinse the lentils and cook in boiling water for 20 minutes until tender but not mushy. Drain and set 6 Main Courses
Cook the rice according to package directions and set aside.
Cook the macaroni until al dente, drain, and set aside.
If using dried chickpeas, soak overnight and boil until tender. If canned, rinse and warm through.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep pan. Fry the sliced onions in batches until crispy and golden brown. Drain on paper towels.
For the tomato sauce, saute minced garlic in a little oil, add tomato sauce, cumin, coriander, chili powder, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 15 minutes.
To assemble, layer rice, lentils, and macaroni in individual bowls. Top with chickpeas and crispy fried onions.

Serve hot with tomato sauce, a splash of vinegar, and hot sauce on the side. 7 Main Courses Mulukhiya A cherished Egyptian soup made from jute mallow leaves, creating a thick, viscous green broth that is deeply flavored with garlic, coriander, and tender meat. This iconic dish is a staple at every Egyptian family table. Calories: 320 kcal Serves: 6 people Time: 2 hours Ingredients 500g fresh or frozen jute mallow leaves (molokhia) 2 bay leaves 500g beef or lamb chunks with bone 1 cinnamon stick 6 cups beef or chicken broth 1/2 tsp black pepper 8 cloves garlic, minced Salt to taste 2 tbsp ground coriander Fresh lemon for serving 2 tbsp butter or ghee White rice for serving 1 large onion, quartered Baladi bread for serving Instructions
Boil the meat chunks with onion, bay leaves, cinnamon stick, salt, and pepper in 8 cups of water for 90 minutes until tender. Strain to make broth. Reserve the meat. 8 Main Courses
If using fresh molokhia leaves, wash thoroughly, remove stems, and finely chop or blend until finely minced. If frozen, thaw completely.
Bring 6 cups of broth to a gentle boil. Add the molokhia leaves and stir well. Simmer on low heat
